commits: 359a106: test(durability): AC-034a/b power-cut harness + AC-034c blocker
fixtures_added: tests/support/corruption.rs: corrupt_wal_invalid_page_size — writes a 32-byte WAL sidecar with valid magic but a page_size > SQLITE_MAX_PAGE_SIZE
tests/support/corruption.rs: corrupt_embedder_profile_row — sets _fathomdb_embedder_profiles.dimension = -1 so row.get<u32> fails on reopen
tests/support/corruption.rs: wal_sidecar_path — pure helper for the WAL-suffix derivation
substrate_reused: tests/support/corruption.rs: corrupt_database_header (AC-006 page-corruption tool) — reused for the HeaderMalformed fixture
tests/support/corruption.rs: corrupt_interior_page_byte — reused for the SchemaInconsistent fixture
src/lib.rs: existing CorruptionDetail / CorruptionKind / OpenStage / CorruptionLocator / RecoveryHint / EngineOpenError enums (no shape changes)
tests/perf_gates.rs: AGENT_LONG=1 long-run gating pattern reused for durability_soak.rs
engine_changes: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs: open_locked reorder — PRAGMA application_id (HeaderProbe) then SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_schema (SchemaProbe) then probe_wal_sidecar then pragma journal_mode=WAL. Restores 1-to-1 (CorruptionKind, OpenStage, RecoveryHint.code) routing required by AC-035b.

src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs: probe_open_integrity body switched from `PRAGMA schema_version` (header-only) to `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_schema` (b-tree traversal) so page-1 leaf corruption surfaces as SchemaInconsistent instead of being silently passed through.

src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs: new probe_wal_sidecar — engine-owned WAL sidecar magic + page-size pre-check. Real reliability win as well as test substrate: SQLite would otherwise silently discard a malformed WAL and lose committed frames; we now refuse to open with Corruption(WalReplayFailure).

blockers_encountered.description: AC-035c (process introspection) requires /proc/self/{fd,task} which is linux-only.
blockers_encountered.resolution: Gated the two AC-035c tests behind `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`. Matches existing perf_gates.rs runner-pin discipline (linux-only CI gate). No change to the canonical CI tier.

blockers_encountered.description: AC-035c assertion requires that no `fathomdb-*` thread is alive in the failed-open process. Default `cargo test` parallelism would race sibling tests that hold an Engine alive.
blockers_encountered.resolution: Added a static `OnceLock<Mutex<()>>` (`serial_guard`) that every test in `durability_open_path.rs` acquires. Tests in this binary now serialize end-to-end; the AC-035c thread-name probe sees zero residue.

blockers_encountered.description: AC-034c (OS-crash) requires a KVM image with `echo c > /proc/sysrq-trigger` and a preserved disk sync barrier (per `dev/acceptance.md` § AC-034c fixture). That VM substrate does not exist in this repo or `dev/release/fixtures/`. Substituting `kill -9` for an OS crash would be silent AC weakening per `feedback_reliability_principles.md` no-punt rule.
blockers_encountered.resolution: Landed `ac_034c_os_crash_zero_committed_tx_loss` as an `#[ignore]` placeholder whose `ignore` reason carries the blocker text. `cargo test --test durability_soak ac_034c` reports `1 ignored` (visible in CI logs, not a silent skip). Recommended follow-up: 12-D-OS-CRASH slice that lands the KVM image + sysrq trigger first, then clears the `#[ignore]`.

agent_verify_result: pass (after rerunning pre-existing flake `ac_017_vector_projection_freshness_p99_le_five_seconds` once per slice prompt — second run green)

long_run_results.ac_034b_lost_commit_p99_ms: 1 (budget 100; harness reports n=99 surviving trials, min=0, median=0, p99=1)

net_loc_vs_baseline: +736 / -4 across 6 files (all new lines are tests, test helpers, engine probe additions; no production code deletions). Open-path probe additions are net-positive but justified as both AC-035b substrate AND a real reliability fix for SQLite's silent-WAL-drop behavior.
discipline_notes: TDD: AC-035a/b/c tests written red, then engine open_locked reordered green. AC-034a/b harness validated against fresh runs (100/100 trials, p99=1ms).

Inventory pass before writing: confirmed `CorruptionDetail`/`OpenStage`/`CorruptionLocator`/`RecoveryHint`/`EngineOpenError` enums and `corrupt_database_header`/`corrupt_interior_page_byte` helpers already existed. Reused all of them.
No silent AC weakening: AC-034c surfaced as blocker rather than replacing OS-crash with kill-9.
Comment policy followed: WHY-only comments, no `// added for 12-D` markers.
Schema-only changes: no DB migration or data-migration code was added (per feedback_no_data_migration).
next_step_for_orchestrator: promote phase-12-D-durability-harnesses-20260517T185755Z to 0.6.0-rewrite; respawn codex reviewer for verdict; queue 12-D-OS-CRASH follow-up slice for blocker-3 (VM image + sysrq trigger fixture).
